This role values craft, collaboration, and curiosity—and offers the opportunity to help evolve a growing brand that’s all about helping people live rewarded.

Reporting into the Brand Experience team, this is a highly collaborative position that partners across the company. Success in this role comes from clear communication, strong creative judgment, and the ability to connect individual projects back to bigger brand goals and our mission of helping people get more value from the things they do everyday.

This is a full-time role that can be held from one of our US offices or remotely in the United States.

Role Responsibilities:

Write and concept copy across a range of formats—including consumer-facing marketing, partner materials, and internal initiatives—based on project briefs and brand objectives.
Produce clear, compelling, and effective copy that reflects Fetch’s voice and supports both business goals and user delight.
Contribute to the ongoing development and refinement of Fetch’s tone and voice, helping improve and evolve how we communicate.
Build and apply a strong understanding of Fetch users, using insights to inform messaging, ideas, and creative decisions.
Collaborate closely with designers, Brand Experience teammates, and cross-functional partners to deliver cohesive, high-quality work.
Review, refine, and guide AI-generated copy to ensure it meets Fetch standards for voice, tone, and quality—and help improve AI workflows over time.
Proactively identify opportunities for Fetch to show up in relevant cultural moments, seasons, and trends with thoughtful, on-brand ideas.

Minimum Requirements:

3+years of experience writing copy across multiple channels, or equivalent demonstrated experience through a strong portfolio.
The ability to write clear, engaging copy and articulate ideas confidently in both written and verbal contexts.
A portfolio that shows strong craft, conceptual thinking, and work that successfully delivers on a brief or objective.
Experience developing or contributing to a brand’s tone and voice, and comfort working within established guidelines.
A strategic and creative mindset—able to translate insights, feedback, and goals into effective creative work.
Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and comfort managing multiple projects or deadlines.
A collaborative, low-ego approach and the ability to work productively with partners across disciplines.
Experience using AI tools as part of the creative process, or a curiosity and willingness to learn new tools quickly.
Working knowledge of tools like Adobe Creative Cloud and Figma, and experience collaborating within digital asset or workflow management systems.
Curiosity about culture and emerging trends, and the ability to apply those insights thoughtfully to creative work.

Compensation:

At Fetch, we offer competitive compensation packages including base, equity, and benefits to the exceptional folks we hire. The base salary range for this position is [75,324 - 81,970].

At Fetch, we'll give you the tools to feel healthy, happy and secure through:

Equity: We offer employees equity in Fetch, so that everyone can benefit from Fetch’s growth.
401k Match: Dollar-for-dollar match up to 4%.
Benefits for humans and pets: We offer comprehensive medical, dental and vision plans for everyone including your pets.
Continuing Education: Fetch provides ten thousand per year in education reimbursement.
Employee Resource Groups: Take part in employee-led groups that are centered around fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace through events, dialogue and advocacy. The ERGs participate in our Inclusion Council with members of executive leadership.
Paid Time Off: On top of our flexible PTO, Fetch observes 9 paid holidays, as well as our year-end week-long break.
Robust Leave Policies: 20 weeks of paid parental leave for primary caregivers, 14 weeks for secondary caregivers, and a flexible return to work schedule.
Calvin Care Cash: Employees who are welcoming new family members will also receive a one time $2,000 incentive to assist employees with covering the cost of childcare, clothing, diapers and much more!
Flexible Work Environment: Collaborate with your team in one of our stunning offices in Madison, Birmingham, or Chicago. Or you can work fully remotely from anywhere in the US. We’ll ensure you are equally equipped with the hardware and software you need to get your job done in the comfort of your home.
evaluation_reason: The role's US-based remote requirement fundamentally clashes with the essential British Time Zone constraint. Additionally, the company's niche and brand aesthetic do not align with Natalie's specialized experience in wellness/personal development or her desired creative environment.

Launch Potato is a profitable digital media company that reaches over 30M+ monthly visitors through brands such as FinanceBuzz and All About Cookies. The Lead Video Copywriter will elevate the copywriting function by driving performance across paid media while strengthening the brand voice and creative systems.

• Own the brand writing strategy across all paid media channels, ensuring consistent voice, messaging, and positioning
• Develop, refine, and scale positioning playbooks that guide high-performing, conversion-focused copywriting
• Establish creative quality standards for ad copy, landing pages, CTAs, and video scripts to drive stronger channel performance

• Manage a network of freelance writers and collaborators, ensuring high throughput and quality control

job_description:
About Company

N. Richards & Company is a leading wholesale and retail distributor operating across Zimbabwe. With a rich history of serving communities and businesses, we are committed to providing quality products and exceptional service. Our extensive network ensures that we reach diverse markets, contributing to the economic growth and development of the regions we operate in. We pride ourselves on our robust supply chain, customer-centric approach, and a dynamic team dedicated to excellence. As we continue to expand our digital footprint and market reach, we are looking for passionate individuals to join our growing team.

Job Description

Are you a wordsmith with a flair for crafting compelling narratives? N. Richards & Company is seeking a talented and enthusiastic Junior Copywriter to join our dynamic marketing team. This is a fantastic remote opportunity for an emerging talent to make a significant impact from anywhere in Zimbabwe, specifically for someone based in the Gokwe, Midlands Province region, or willing to operate within Zimbabwean time zones. As a Junior Copywriter, you will be instrumental in developing engaging content that resonates with our diverse customer base and strengthens our brand presence across various digital and traditional platforms.

In this role, you will work closely with our marketing and sales teams to create clear, concise, and persuasive copy for a wide range of materials. Your responsibilities will include drafting website content, social media posts, email marketing campaigns, product descriptions, promotional materials, and internal communications. We are looking for someone with a sharp eye for detail, excellent grammar, and a creative mind capable of transforming complex ideas into accessible and captivating text. This position offers a unique chance to grow your skills, receive mentorship, and contribute to a well-established company with a strong commitment to its values and customers. If you're eager to learn, adapt, and bring fresh perspectives to our brand's voice, we encourage you to apply and help us tell our story more effectively.

Key Responsibilities
• Develop and write clear, concise, and compelling copy for various marketing channels, including websites, blogs, social media, email campaigns, and advertisements.
• Collaborate with the marketing team to understand project requirements and target audience to tailor content effectively.
• Conduct thorough research on industry-related topics, keywords, and competitors to inform content creation.
• Proofread and edit copy for grammar, spelling, punctuation, and brand consistency.
• Ensure all content adheres to brand guidelines, tone of voice, and legal requirements.
• Assist in brainstorming and developing creative content strategies and campaigns.
• Manage multiple writing assignments and deadlines efficiently.
• Learn and apply SEO best practices to optimize content for search engines.
• Participate in content review meetings and incorporate feedback effectively.

Required Skills
• Exceptional written and verbal communication skills in English.
• Strong understanding of grammar, punctuation, and style.
• Ability to craft engaging and persuasive copy.
• Proficiency in research and fact-checking.
• Basic knowledge of digital marketing principles and content creation for various platforms.
• Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
• Ability to work independently and manage time effectively in a remote setting.
• Familiarity with standard office software (e.g., Microsoft Office Suite, Google Docs).

Preferred Qualifications
• A diploma or degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, English, or a related field.
• Previous experience (internship or entry-level) in copywriting or content creation, preferably with a portfolio.
• Familiarity with SEO principles and content optimization tools.
• Experience with content management systems (CMS).
• Understanding of the retail or wholesale industry.

Perks & Benefits
• Competitive monthly salary.
• Fully remote work model, offering flexibility and work-life balance.
• Opportunity to work with a reputable and well-established company.
• Mentorship and professional development opportunities to grow your skills.
• Supportive and collaborative team environment.
• Contribution to meaningful projects that impact local communities.
• Access to a comprehensive suite of digital tools and resources for remote collaboration.

How to Apply

Interested candidates are invited to submit their application, including a resume and a cover letter detailing their experience and interest in this role, by clicking the link below to apply. Please highlight any relevant writing samples or portfolio links.
evaluation_reason: This Junior Copywriter role is a significant mismatch for your 13+ years of senior-level strategic and launch copywriting experience, desired wellness/personal development niche, and specific British time zone constraint. While remote, the company's industry and location do not align with your aesthetic or quality standards.

job_title: Copywriter job at Grifols in Raleigh, NC, Durham, NC, Chapel Hill, NC
job_description:
Title: Copywriter

Location: : NC-RTP || NORTH AMERICA : USA : NC-Durham || NORTH AMERICA : USA : NC-Raleigh:USNC0001 - RTP NC-Headquarters

Req Id: 540039

Work Type: Hybrid

Job Description:

Would you like to join an international team working to improve the future of healthcare? Do you want to enhance the lives of millions of people? Grifols is a global healthcare company that since 1909 has been working to improve the health and well-being of people around the world. We are leaders in plasma-derived medicines and transfusion medicine and develop, produce and market innovative medicines, solutions and services in more than 110 countries and regions.

Location: Hybrid - Based in our RTP office

Work Arrangement: This role offers a hybrid schedule, with a mix of remote work and in-office collaboration. Candidates should be in the Raleigh, Durham, Chapel Hill, NC area.

We are looking for a highly skilled and strategic Copywriter to join our dynamic marketing team. This role is central to shaping and maintaining our brand voice and driving customer engagement through compelling, relatable content across all B2C channels. The ideal candidate has a proven track record of transforming complex product features into accessible, emotionally resonant narratives that motivate action and build lasting customer relationships.

PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES FOR ROLE:

Content Strategy Execution: Lead the creation of strategic B2C copy that aligns with brand guidelines, campaign objectives, and overall business goals.
Relatable Storytelling: Develop high-performing, emotionally resonant copy for a variety of channels, including:
Email Marketing: Subject lines, body copy, and CTA's designed for segmentation and conversion.
Social Media: Organic and paid social ads (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, etc.) with a focus on platform-native tone and consumer insight.
Advertising: Scripts for video/audio, banner ads, and print advertising.
Digital/Web: Landing pages, website copy, product descriptions, and in-app messaging.
Customer-Centric Approach: Deeply understand our target audience's pain points, aspirations, and language, translating these insights into compelling content that solves problems and creates connection.
A/B Testing & Optimization: Collaborate with design and analytics teams to implement rigorous A/B testing, analyze copy performance, and iterate quickly to maximize conversion and engagement rates.
Brand Voice Champion: Maintain, evolve, and enforce a consistent, distinct, and approachable brand voice across all external communications.

ADDITIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES:

Review, edit, and provide final approval for all B2C copies developed by external agencies, internal teams, and channel partners to ensure strict brand alignment, tonal consistency, and regulatory compliance.
Serve as the primary subject matter expert for copy and narrative strategy in cross-functional working groups offering proactive guidance to mitigate messaging risks.
Oversee the organization and maintenance of key messaging matrices, campaign boilerplate copy, and approved claim libraries for easy access by marketing and sales teams.
Proactively monitor competitor communication and advertising strategies to identify market opportunities, messaging gaps, and potential areas for differentiation.

This job description is intended to present the general content and requirements for the performance of this job. The description is not to be construed as an exhaustive statement of duties, responsibilities, or requirements. Managers and supervisors may assign other duties as needed.

EDUCATION

Degree in one of the following:

English or Literature: Focuses on narrative structure, tone, grammar, and creative writing.
Journalism: Emphasizes clear, concise, and persuasive communication, often under tight deadlines.
Marketing, Communications, or Advertising: Provides a foundation in consumer behavior, strategic messaging, and integrated marketing campaigns.

Equivalency: Depending on the area of assignment, directly related experience or a combination of directly related education and experience and/or competencies may be considered in place of the stated requirements. Example: If a job level requires a Bachelor's degree plus 4 years of experience, an equivalency could include 8 years of experience, an Associate's degree with 6 years of experience, or a Master's degree with 2 years of experience.

EXPERIENCE

5+ years of professional copywriting experience, with a significant portfolio showcasing success in high-volume, performance-driven B2C environments (e.g., e-commerce, tech, DTC)
Experience in a regulated industry (e.g., Finance, Health, CPG) that requires balancing compliance with creativity.
Proficiency in SEO best practices and content optimization.
Familiarity with marketing automation platforms.

KNOWLEDGE | SKILLS | ABILITIES

Expert B2C Persuasion: Advanced skill in writing high-converting, empathetic copy tailored to drive specific consumer actions across the entire marketing funnel.
Channel Mastery: Proficiency in adapting tone, format, and style for optimal performance across all major B2C channels (e.g., social, email, web, ads).
A/B Testing & Optimization: Proven skill in designing, executing, and interpreting A/B and multivariate copy tests to continuously improve conversion and engagement rates.
Strategic Brief Translation: The ability to rapidly translate complex business objectives, product features, and technical details into simple, compelling consumer benefits.
Brand and Compliance Stewardship: Expert knowledge and ability to enforce brand voice and style while ensuring copy adheres to relevant legal and regulatory guidelines.
Audience Empathy: Innate ability to adopt the consumer's mindset to create deeply relatable messaging that addresses pain points and aspirations.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Strong skill in clearly articulating creative rationale and working seamlessly with design, product, and channel marketing teams.
Meticulous Craftsmanship: Exceptional skill in self-editing, proofreading, and delivering flawless final copy under tight deadlines.
Platform Trend Fluency & Relevance: Proven ability to monitor and analyze emerging trends, language, and cultural shifts on major B2C platforms (e.g., TikTok, Instagram, YouTube) and provide strategic directions to adapt brand voice and copy execution, ensuring Grifols' messaging remains relatable, authentic, and culturally relevant to its target audience.

Agencies that present a candidate to Grifols must have an active, nonexpired, Grifols Agency Master Services Agreement with the Grifols Talent Acquisition Department. Additionally, agencies may only submit candidates to positions that they have been engaged to work on by a Grifols Recruiter. All resumes must be sent to a Grifols Recruiter under these terms or they will be considered a Grifols candidate.

We will consider for employment all qualified applicants in a manner consistent with the requirements of all applicable laws

job_title: Digital Copywriter job at Solomon Page in Philadelphia, PA
job_description:
Digital Copywriter

EMPLOYEE TYPE: Contract

WORKPLACE: Remote

Job Description

Our telecommunications client is seeking a detail-oriented freelance Mid–Senior Level Copywriter to support a large-scale website migration initiative. This role will play a critical part in ensuring all migrated content aligns with updated brand guidelines, reflects strategic structural changes, and maintains clarity and consistency across the user experience.

The ideal candidate is equally comfortable creating new copy, editing, and rewriting copy as needed. You will collaborate closely with IA and Digital Strategy partners to understand site architecture changes and proactively adjust messaging to preserve intent, SEO value, and user clarity.

Rate: $50-$55/hr. (DOE)
Location: Philadelphia, PA, HYBRID

Responsibilities:

Review all website pages slated for migration to ensure alignment with updated brand guidelines and messaging standards.
Rewrite or refine copy when sections or elements are being removed, consolidated, or restructured.
Develop clear, comprehensive primary reference documents when direction is not fully defined within design files (Figma).
Partner with Information Architecture (IA) and Digital Strategy teams to understand structural changes and their impact on content.
Revise content strategically based on page removals, navigation updates, or consolidation efforts.
Ensure messaging consistency, tone of voice, and brand alignment across all revised pages.
Identify gaps in content or messaging created by structural changes and proactively recommend solutions.
Maintain strong attention to detail across copy, headlines, calls-to-action, and supporting content.

Required Qualifications:

4+ years of copywriting experience, ideally in digital environments.
Strong experience supporting website migrations, redesigns, or large-scale content audits.
Proven ability to adapt messaging to updated brand guidelines.
Experience collaborating with IA, UX, or Digital Strategy teams.
Comfortable working from Figma files and translating design intent into clear, strategic copy.
Strong editorial skills with a keen eye for clarity, hierarchy, and consistency.
Ability to manage multiple pages and revisions simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.
Experience developing structured content documentation or messaging frameworks.

If you meet the required qualifications and are interested in this role, please apply today.

The Solomon Page Distinction

Solomon Page offers a comprehensive benefit program for hourly employees. We pride ourselves on offering medical, dental, 401(k), direct deposit and commuter benefits to our employees, including freelancers – which sets us apart in the industries we serve.

Job Description:

At Solventum, we enable better, smarter, safer healthcare to improve lives. As a new company with a long legacy of creating breakthrough solutions for our customers’ toughest challenges, we pioneer game-changing innovations at the intersection of health, material and data science that change patients' lives for the better while enabling healthcare professionals to perform at their best. Because people, and their wellbeing, are at the heart of every scientific advancement we pursue.

We partner closely with the brightest minds in healthcare to ensure that every solution we create melds the latest technology with compassion and empathy. Because at Solventum, we never stop solving for you.

The impact you’ll make in this role
As a Senior Copywriter within Solventum’s budding internal agency, Solve Studio, you’ll be a key storyteller for the enterprise, bringing patient, provider and employee experiences to life through inspired video scripts, digital campaigns, event concepts and more.

You’ll work closely with Communications & Corporate Marketing and business stakeholders to produce unique, clear and on-brand creative copy that meets functional business needs and enterprise brand requirements, ultimately contributing to driving brand awareness, trust and advocacy and moving current and potential customers to explore and purchase our products.

But this role isn’t just about converting customers. Your work will showcase the true potential of purpose-driven creativity, delivering smart, intentional solutions that earn trust and belief from healthcare professionals, the patients they care for and the solvers who represent us. Together, we’ll elevate how Solventum shows up in the world, bringing a heartbeat to our brand and raising the quality of every touchpoint, experience and moment that matters.

Key responsibilities
• Produce copywriting projects of broad scope and high complexity, from creative ideation to execution, for internal and external storytelling across both the enterprise and our businesses
• Develop high-quality, engaging, unique and persuasive content for a variety of channels including digital, social, events and experiential
• Maintain expert knowledge of Solventum’s verbal identity, ensuring all content embodies our personality and tone, as well as our technical style
• Edit and proofread content meticulously, maintaining the highest standards of accuracy and quality
• Consult with design partners and account managers as business stakeholders submit requests for support to ensure alignment on needed outputs
• Utilize provided productivity tools, like AI and automation, to efficiently manage and perform key work responsibilities
• Utilize a work management system to accept, track and complete requests from business partners
• Coach and mentor other writers as needed

• Bachelor’s degree
• At least 8 years of proven experience as a copywriter, demonstrated through a strong portfolio of work
• Exceptional writing, editing and proofreading skills with an outstanding command of the English language
• Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment, including potential nights and weekends (up to 25% of the time) to meet deadlines

Preferred qualifications
• Experience writing for the healthcare, medical device or life sciences industry
• Experience working within a creative agency
• Comfort incorporating new technologies and automation tools in everyday processes
• Ability to work in a team environment in a role that requires flexibility and collaboration and a quality driven mindset
• Strong prioritization, communication and execution skills and transformational/change mindset

Work location: Remote

Travel: Up to 10%

Relocation Assistance: Not approved

Must be legally authorized to work in country of employment without sponsorship for employment visa status (e.g., H1B status).

Supporting Your Well-being

Solventum offers many programs to help you live your best life – both physically and financially. To ensure competitive pay and benefits, Solventum regularly benchmarks with other companies that are comparable in size and scope.

job_description:
Company Overview:

When it comes to IT solution providers, there are a lot of choices. But when it comes to providers with innovative and differentiating end-to-end service offerings, there’s really only one: Zones – First Choice for IT.TM

Zones is a Global Solution Provider of end-to-end IT solutions with an unmatched supply chain. Positioned to be the IT partner you need, Zones, a Minority Business Enterprise (MBE) in business for over 35 years, specializes in Digital Workplace, Cloud & Data Center, Networking, Security, and Managed/Professional/Staffing services. Operating in more than 120 countries, leveraging a robust portfolio, and utilizing the highest certification levels from key partners, including Microsoft, Apple, Cisco, Lenovo, Adobe, and more, Zones has mastered the science of building digital infrastructures that change the way business does business ensuring whatever they need, they can Consider IT Done. Follow Zones, LLC on Twitter @Zones, and LinkedIn and Facebook.

Position Overview:

We are seeking a skilled and experienced Copywriter I to join our marketing team. As a Copywriter, you will be responsible for crafting compelling and engaging copy for various marketing channels and campaigns. You will work to ensure all content is on-brand, consistent in style, quality-driven, and aligns with our company's messaging, tone and voice, and company goals. The ideal candidate should have a proven track record of creating impactful copy, strong communication skills, and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment.

The position is remote based in the United States, with a preferred location in Seattle or Chicago.

What you will do as a Copywriter I:

The requirements below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. This job description is not intended to be a comprehensive list of the duties and responsibilities of the position, and the duties and responsibilities may change without notice.

• Conceptualize and develop creative and persuasive copy for marketing campaigns, advertisements, websites, emails, social media, corporate collateral, and other channels.
• Collaborate closely with designers, Integrated Marketing, Website team, and other teams to understand project requirements, target audience, and objectives.
• Edit and proofread copy to ensure clarity, grammar, consistency, and brand voice and tone adherence.
• Understand and follow AP Style Guides for copy consistency and adherence.
• Stay updated on industry trends and best practices in copywriting and digital marketing. Knowledge of the IT technology industry is a bonus.
• Conduct research and interviews to understand customer needs and market trends.
• Follow an established content calendar to ensure timely delivery of materials.
• Follow an established copy standards playbook to adhere to content consistency.

What you will bring to the team:

• 1+ years of proven experience as a Copywriter in B2B technology companies, IT/AV distribution or reseller preferred.
• Exceptional writing, editing, and proofreading skills.
• Ability to adapt writing style to various tones, voices, and platforms.
• Excellent organizational and time-management skills.
• Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, or related field (preferred).
• Proficiency in SEO principles and content optimization.
• Excellent project management and organizational skills with the ability to prioritize and multitask in a fast-paced environment.
• Proven ability to work closely and successfully with a sales organization.
• Ability to work very independently and collaborate with cross-functional teams.
• Rigorous attention to detail and focus on quality of deliverables.
• Strong communication and interpersonal skills to build relationships across sales, marketing and external vendors and associates.
Qualified candidates can expect a hourly rate of $24.03.

While we’re committed to providing top-tier solutions, we’re just as committed to supporting our own team. Our team members enjoy a variety of comprehensive benefits, including medical, dental and vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, voluntary accident, hospitalization and critical illness insurance options, a 401(k) plan with matching provision, and many more. Generally, Zones currently offers paid time off and personal sick leave in compliance to individual state requirements.

At Zones, work is more than a job – it is an exciting career on a global team that is client centric, has a passion for technology, embraces change and lifelong learning in a collaborative culture. And as a Minority Business Enterprise, a Corporate Plus member of the Northwest Minority Supplier Development Council, and an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer, our community is just as diverse. job_title: Senior Copywriter | ezCater, Inc | Remote (United States)
job_description:
ezCater is the leading food for work technology company in the US, connecting anyone who needs food for their workplace to over 100,000 restaurants nationwide. For workplaces, ezCater provides flexible and scalable solutions for everything from recurring employee meals to one-off meetings, all backed by 24/7 customer service with real humans. ezCater also enables companies to manage their food spend in a single, customizable platform. For restaurant partners, ezCater helps them grow their business by bringing them more orders and new high-value customers. To learn more, visit www.ezcater.com.

As a Senior Copywriter at ezCater, you’ll be part of a small but mighty (and growing) in-house creative team. You’ll play a central role in our customer marketing across digital, email, collateral, out-of-home and more. You’ll also be joining us at an exciting time as we refine our brand to complement our company’s growth.

If most of this sounds like you, we’d love to talk:

You’re a writer. A thinker. An idea person. A communicator.

You’re a craftsperson who can write with clarity and warmth (a little humor is always welcome, too).

You’re someone who knows when to use a complete sentence. And when to use a fragment.

You’ll be excited to learn what makes our customers tick, and write to their needs in every channel.

What You’ll Do:

Reporting to our Principal Writer, and working alongside Design and Video within the Brand team, you will:

• Come to know our personas on both sides of our marketplace — their wants and needs, their fears and frustrations, how to reach them, and what they need to hear.

• Understand our brand, our products, and our messaging inside and out, figuring out what we want and need to say, and when and how to say it.

• Write short – and long-form copy that is on-brand, concise, persuasive, and action-inspiring — ads that grab attention, landing pages that convert, and emails that people open and read.

• Experiment with messaging and prove which words work. Celebrate the wins, learn from the flops, and never stop improving.

• Help clarify and evolve our brand voice.

• Elevate the quality of writing throughout ezCater.

• Learn from — and teach — a first-rate team of creative professionals.

What You Have:

• 5-7 years of experience writing copy and conceptual work for B2C and B2B companies

• Inventiveness, so what you write is engaging even while it’s plainspoken’

• Strong conceptual and critical thinking skills

• Deep knowledge of and appreciation for language and the power of words to communicate the essence of a brand and build relationships with customers

• The capacity to think multiple steps ahead, so what you write is compelling in the moment but also furthers the lifelong conversation we’re in with our audience

• A “get it done — right” mentality, where your natural attention to detail is balanced with a history of delivering high-quality work on deadline

• The ability to collaborate and share ideas, then work autonomously and manage your time and projects

• An eagerness and willingness to accept and apply constructive feedback — we all love learning from each other here

• The ability to communicate clearly, thoroughly, and succinctly orally and in writing – you can describe and defend a concept

• The ability to actively contribute to strategic brainstorming and campaign development

• A portfolio that shows off your thought processes and writing chops

The national cash compensation range for this role is $87,000 – $115,000*

*Please note: Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors, including prior experience, expertise and region & may vary from the amount above. This range does not represent additional compensation benefits (such as equity, 401K or medical, dental or vision insurance).

Please have fun with the Cover Letter portion of the application! It does not need to follow “traditional” cover letter guidelines – we would love for you to write 150-500 words explaining why you are interested in ezCater and the role, and highlighting anything else you think we should know!

ezCater does not sponsor applicants for work visas or legal permanent residence.

What You’ll Get from Us:

You’ll get a terrifically compelling opportunity, in an environment of radical transparency, and collaborative colleagues at every level of our organization. You’ll also get sane working hours and great flexibility around work/life balance.

Have people in your life – of any age – who always, often, or sometimes need your help? We make room for that. Have a bad thing or a good thing happen to you? We make room for that, too.

Oh, and you’ll get all this: Market salary, stock options that you’ll help make worth a lot, the usual holidays, all-you-can-eat vacation, 401K with ezCater match, health/dental/FSA, long-term disability insurance, remote-hybrid work from our awesome Boston or Denver offices OR your home OR a mixture of both home and office (you choose!), a tremendous amount of responsibility and autonomy, wicked awesome co-workers, cupcakes (and many more goodies) when you’re in one of our offices, and knowing that you helped get this rocket ship to the moon.

ezCater is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace humans of every background, appearance, race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, and disability status. At the same time, we do not employ jerks, even brilliant ones.

For information on how ezCater collects and uses job applicants’ personal information, please visit our Job Applicant Privacy Policy.

Iteration: You revise your work according to the feedback and resubmit.
Approval: Once approved, you move on to the next deliverable.
All communication, briefs, work in progress, and final deliverables are tracked transparently.
Compensation $5 per accepted deliverable
Most copywriters complete 10-15 deliverables per hour ($50–$75/hour possible for experienced, efficient writers)

Those comfortable with high-volume, iterative writing tasks

Complete a short, unpaid test deliverable (part of the application process)
If your test meets standards, you’ll be invited to start paid work immediately

Training and feedback from experienced Creative Directors included

job_description:
At BairesDev®, we've been leading the way in technology projects for over 15 years. We deliver cutting-edge solutions to giants like Google and the most innovative startups in Silicon Valley.

Our diverse 4,000+ team, composed of the world's Top 1% of tech talent, works remotely on roles that drive significant impact worldwide.

When you apply for this position, you're taking the first step in a process that goes beyond the ordinary. We aim to align your passions and skills with our vacancies, setting you on a path to exceptional career development and success.

Technical Copywriter (Backend) at BairesDev

We need a part-time, contract Technical Copywriter focused on backend engineering topics for our blog. Output is 1 to 2 long-form ghostwritten articles per month, written for senior technical audiences and optimized for SEO without sounding generic or promotional. You must be able to research complex backend concepts, validate claims, and translate engineering reality into clear, structured articles with practical examples. Scope is backend broadly: APIs, services, data stores, performance, reliability, security basics, and production operations.

What You'll Do:
• Write 2–3 backend-focused long-form blog articles per month, typically 1,500–2,500 words unless otherwise specified.
• Convert complex backend topics into practical, readable guidance with clear structure, examples, and trade-offs.
• Ensure technical correctness by validating terminology, architecture claims, performance/security statements, and gotchas.
• Incorporate SEO fundamentals naturally, including search intent alignment, headings, internal linking suggestions, and meta title/description suggestions if requested.
• Revise drafts based on editorial feedback while preserving accuracy and clarity.

What we are looking for:
• Proven experience writing long-form technical content for engineering audiences, such as blog/editorial, not brand copy.
• Backend technical fluency from hands-on engineering experience.
• Ability to independently research, sanity-check, and cite sources while avoiding unverified claims.
• Clear, direct written English suitable for a professional engineering blog.

Must-haves:
• Proven ability to write long-form backend engineering content for senior technical audiences. Portfolio required.
• Demonstrated senior-level thinking: can explain trade-offs, constraints, and failure modes, not just definitions.
• Deep expertise in 2–3 backend domains such as API design, distributed systems, databases/data modeling, performance/reliability, observability, or messaging/queues.
• Ability to research efficiently and maintain technical accuracy with no unsupported claims or invented metrics.

Join a global team where your unique talents can truly thrive and make a significant impact!

Apply now!

Drop is a data-driven product design company built by and for enthusiasts. job_title: Lead Copywriter job at Darn Tough Vermont in Waterbury, VT
job_description:
Lead Copywriter - Waterbury, VT

Job Category: MARKETING

Requisition Number: LEADC001469

Posting Details

Full-Time

Locations

Waterbury
Darn Tough Vermont
150 Pilgrim Park Rd
Waterbury, VT 05676, USA

Job Details

Description

The Company:

Founded in 1978, Cabot Hosiery Mills is an American manufacturer of premium socks with uncompromised comfort, durability, and fit. With a family of brands including Darn Tough Vermont, Wide Open, and Cabot & Sons, our mission is to make the world’s best socks, in Vermont, USA, and back them with our Unconditional Lifetime Guarantee. We are family owned, three generations strong, and have yet to produce our best sock.

Job Summary:

As we continue to scale one of the fastest growing apparel brands, we are seeking a strategic, hands-on Lead Copywriter to set messaging direction and define how the brand communicates and tells its story across campaigns and consumer touchpoints. This role owns messaging strategy, concept development, and copy standards; ensuring brand voice and tone are clear, cohesive, and impactful across all initiatives. The role provides daily guidance, feedback and instruction to lower level copywriters to ensure brand voice is consistent and effective.

Working in close partnership with the Director of Content + Creative, Manager of Corporate Communications, and creative, marketing, and cross-functional teams, the Lead Copywriter shapes and evolves the brand’s voice, narrative, and messaging frameworks to support brand and business objectives.

The Lead Copywriter leads copy from concept through execution, setting the standard for quality, consistency, and effectiveness. This role mentor’s copywriters, manages workflows, and elevates the overall strength, cohesion, and strategic impact of brand messaging.

the starting salary for this role is $90,000-$105,000 based on experience

Key Responsibilities:

Messaging Strategy & Creative Development

Lead messaging concept development and strategy for brand and product campaigns across all channels.
Establish, shape, evolve, and uphold brand voice, tone, messaging standards, and copy systems to ensure consistency and clarity.
Translate business and marketing objectives into compelling messaging frameworks and creative concepts.
Lead copy development from initial concept through execution, ensuring alignment with approved creative direction.
Clearly articulate messaging rationale, decisions, and strategic intent to internal teams and stakeholders.
Contribute to naming, storytelling, product messaging, campaign narratives, and brand defining initiatives.
Ensure messaging maintains cohesion and impact across all channels.

Team Mentorship

Provide direction, mentorship, and feedback to copywriters, fostering growth in craft, creative thinking, and strategic messaging.
Delegate work effectively across internal and freelance resources, ensuring quality, consistency, and efficiency.
Foster a collaborative, solutions-oriented environment that encourages creative excellence and accountability.
Proactively manage feedback, resolve challenges, and maintain smooth messaging workflows across projects.

Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement

Partner closely with the Director of Content + Creative, Design Lead, Manager of Corporate Communications, Marketing, and Project Management to deliver cohesive, on-brand copy.
Collaborate with designers, content creators, and external partners to ensure alignment between messaging and visual execution.
Present messaging concepts clearly and confidently, incorporating feedback while maintaining messaging integrity.
Support leadership, including Founder and strategic initiatives, with thoughtful, brand aligned messaging.
Partner with Project Management to ensure copy deliverables are completed on time, on brief, and on brand.

Brand Stewardship & Quality

Own messaging decisions and executional standards, ensuring all copy aligns with established brand voice and messaging frameworks.
Maintain consistency and cohesion across creative outputs, identifying and resolving messaging inconsistencies.
Continuously refine and elevate messaging standards, processes, and copy output quality.
Contribute to the evolution of brand narrative and messaging systems as the brand grows and expands.

Qualifications:

7–10+ years of professional copywriting experience, with a strong portfolio of brand and campaign work.
A bachelor’s degree in a relevant field or equivalent experience
Proven experience leading messaging strategy and concept development for large-scale, multi-channel campaigns.
Mastery of short and long form copy, including campaign messaging, storytelling, product copy, naming, and brand narratives.
Demonstrated experience mentoring and developing copywriters and freelancers.
Strong ability to translate strategy into clear, compelling messaging frameworks and executions.
Experience collaborating cross-functionally with designers, marketers, leadership, and external partners.
Strong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to articulate messaging strategy and creative rationale.
Experience working in fast-paced, deadline-driven environments managing multiple priorities simultaneously.
Familiarity with Adobe Creative Suite, Figma, and modern creative workflows.
Highly organized, collaborative, proactive, and solutions oriented.

Success in this Role Looks Like

Messaging across all channels is clear, cohesive, compelling, and consistently aligned with brand voice and standards.
Providing direction and feedback to junior staff members
Campaign messaging is strategically sound, creatively strong, and executed effectively across touchpoints.
Copywriters are supported, growing, and delivering high-quality work aligned with brand standards.
Messaging frameworks improve efficiency, consistency, and creative impact across teams.
Creative and marketing partners view this role as a trusted messaging leader and collaborator.
Copy execution elevates brand perception while supporting campaigns that drive engagement, connection, and measurable business results.
Messaging direction helps define and strengthen the brand narrative as the company grows.

Working Conditions:

Office Environment – some exposure to manufacturing environment
Hybrid schedule – ability to be on site at least 3 days a week
Flexible nature to manage competing and changing priorities
Occasional travel on as needed basis

At Cabot Hosiery Mills, we have a list of shared company values – one of them is We Value Differences. Where you come from, how you identify, your age, and other descriptions are something that makes you, you, and we respect that. We’re an honest group of people and work hard, if you like that, we encourage you to apply.

This job description should not be interpreted as a complete list of all the duties, qualifications and responsibilities performed by the jobholder. To maintain organizational flexibility, the organization has the discretion to add, drop or change at any time the duties, responsibilities and expectations of this position. This job description does not constitute an offer of employment, continuous employment or an employment contract. Reasonable accommodation will be considered to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions of this position.

Equal Opportunity Employer

job_title: Copywriter job at Charlie Health in New York, NY
job_description:
Title: Copywriter

Location: New York ,United States

Job Description:

Why Charlie Health?

Millions of people across the country are navigating mental health conditions, substance use disorders, and eating disorders, but too often, they’re met with barriers to care. From limited local options and long wait times to treatment that lacks personalization, behavioral healthcare can leave people feeling unseen and unsupported.

Charlie Health exists to change that. Our mission is to connect the world to life-saving behavioral health treatment. We deliver personalized, virtual care rooted in connection—between clients and clinicians, care teams, loved ones, and the communities that support them. By focusing on people with complex needs, we’re expanding access to meaningful care and driving better outcomes from the comfort of home.

As a rapidly growing organization, we're reaching more communities every day and building a team that’s redefining what behavioral health treatment can look like. If you're ready to use your skills to drive lasting change and help more people access the care they deserve, we’d love to meet you.

About the Role

Charlie Health is seeking a Product Marketing Copywriter to support the development of high-impact, clarity-driven content across our marketing ecosystem. This role will partner closely with Product Marketing, Design and Growth teams to translate complex behavioral health programs into clear, compelling, and audience-appropriate copy.

You’ll be responsible for writing and refining copy across website pages, whitepapers, case studies, printable assets, and sales collateral, ensuring our value is communicated with empathy, precision, and consistency. This is a highly collaborative role ideal for a writer who enjoys working at the intersection of storytelling and healthcare.

The ideal candidate is a strong writer and editor who can adapt tone across audiences (clients, caregivers, providers, partners), synthesize feedback from multiple stakeholders, and bring structure and polish to both short- and long-form content. Experience in healthcare, behavioral health, or regulated industries is a plus, but strong product marketing instincts and editorial judgment are essential.

We’re a team of passionate, forward-thinking professionals eager to take on the challenge of the mental health crisis and play a formative role in providing life-saving solutions. If you’re inspired by our mission and energized by the opportunity to increase access to mental healthcare and impact millions of lives in a profound way, apply today.

Responsibilities

Write and edit high-quality copy for website pages, including program overviews, condition pages, and conversion-oriented content
Develop long-form assets such as whitepapers, outcomes reports, and case studies that balance narrative storytelling with data and proof points
Support creation of printable and field assets (rack cards, trifolds, one-pagers, two-pagers) in partnership with PMM and Design
Translate Product Marketing strategy, positioning, and packaging frameworks into clear, compelling copy across formats
Adapt messaging for multiple audiences, including clients, caregivers, providers, referral partners, and internal teams
Collaborate cross-functionally with PMMs, designers, clinicians, and leadership to incorporate feedback and ensure accuracy
Edit and refine existing content to improve clarity, flow, tone, and consistency with brand guidelines
Maintain a strong understanding of Charlie Health’s programs, audiences, and differentiators to ensure messaging accuracy and alignment
Support PMM workflows by helping scale output across multiple initiatives and launches simultaneously

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, English, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
2+ years of experience in copywriting, content marketing, or product marketing, preferably in healthcare, mental health, or another regulated industry
Demonstrated experience writing both short-form and long-form content, including websites, whitepapers, case studies, or sales collateral
Strong ability to adapt tone and messaging across different audiences and use cases
Excellent editorial judgment with a proven ability to simplify complex topics without losing nuance
Comfort working cross-functionally and incorporating feedback from multiple stakeholders
Please note that members of this team who live within 45 minutes of a Charlie Health office are expected to adhere to a hybrid work schedule

Benefits

Charlie Health is pleased to offer comprehensive benefits to all full-time, exempt employees. Read more about our benefits here.

The total target base compensation for this role will be between $75,000 and $100,000 per year at the commencement of employment. In addition to base compensation, this role also offers a performance bonus. The expected total cash compensation range, including potential bonus, will be between $82,000 and $116,000 per year. Please note, pay will be determined on an individualized basis and will be impacted by location, experience, expertise, internal pay equity, and other relevant business considerations. Further, cash compensation is only part of the total compensation package, which, depending on the position, may include stock options and other Charlie Health-sponsored benefits. #LI-HYBRID

Our Values

Connection: Care deeply & inspire hope.
Congruence: Stay curious & heed the evidence.
Commitment: Act with urgency & don’t give up.

Please do not call our public clinical admissions line in regard to this or any other job posting.

Please be cautious of potential recruitment fraud. If you are interested in exploring opportunities at Charlie Health, please go directly to our Careers Page: https://www.charliehealth.com/careers/current-openings. Charlie Health will never ask you to pay a fee or download software as part of the interview process with our company. In addition, Charlie Health will not ask for your personal banking information until you have signed an offer of employment and completed onboarding paperwork that is provided by our People Operations team. All communications with Charlie Health Talent and People Operations professionals will only be sent from @charliehealth.com email addresses. Legitimate emails will never originate from gmail.com, yahoo.com, or other commercial email services.

Recruiting agencies, please do not submit unsolicited referrals for this or any open role. We have a roster of agencies with whom we partner, and we will not pay any fee associated with unsolicited referrals.

At Charlie Health, we value being an Equal Opportunity Employer. We strive to cultivate an environment where individuals can be their authentic selves. Being an Equal Opportunity Employer means every member of our team feels as though they are supported and belong. We value diverse perspectives to help us provide essential mental health and substance use disorder treatments to all young people.

Charlie Health applicants are assessed solely on their qualifications for the role, without regard to disability or need for accommodation.

By clicking "Submit application" below, you agree to Charlie Health's Privacy Policy and Terms of Service.

By submitting your application, you agree to receive SMS messages from Charlie Health regarding your application. Message and data rates may apply. Message frequency varies. You can reply STOP to opt out at any time. For help, reply HELP

job_description:
#WeAreParamount on a mission to unleash the power of content… you in?

We've got the brands, we've got the stars, we've got the power to achieve our mission to entertain the planet – now all we're missing is… YOU! Becoming a part of Paramount means joining a team of passionate people who not only recognize the power of content but also enjoy a touch of fun and uniqueness. Together, we co-create moments that matter – both for our audiences and our employees – and aim to leave a positive mark on culture.

Overview:

The Paramount+ Creative Marketing Team seeks a Sports Copywriter to write, proofread and edit various assets that position Paramount+ as a leader in premium sports content with various audiences.

Responsibilities:

• Craft text copy for marquees and show pages.

• Write subject lines, pre-headers and body copy for various subscriber newsletters, as well as non-subscriber communications.

• Create performance, paid social and affiliate emails for various campaigns, series and live games and sporting events.

• Write audio ad copy for priority games and matches, with a proficient focus on UFC numbered fights.

• Create new and engaging strategies to communicate with our audience from push notifications to in-app messages.

• Help occasionally with additional marketing non-sports related requests, including marketing copy for News, CBS, and Paramount+ Originals.

• Editorialize and use storytelling to engage with fans about their favorite teams, coaches and rivalries.

Basic Qualifications:

• 5+ years' demonstrated experience (not including internships) in copywriting, editing, proofreading, media and marketing.

• Experience with working within a brand guide and AP Style guidelines, as well as the ability to apply, maintain and application style guidelines.

• Reports on-site five days a week either in NYC or LA office.

• Minimal travel required.

Additional Qualifications:

• Deep interest for all things sports, especially UFC, UEFA, NFL, PGA, college football and basketball.

• Highly organized collaborator who thrives in dynamic environments, juggling multiple deadlines across diverse teams.

• Meticulous attention to detail with a commitment to accuracy and quality.

• Proficient writer and communicator with the ability to convey ideas clearly, confidently, and appropriately.

Paramount Skydance Corporation (NASDAQ: PSKY) is a leading global media and entertainment company that creates premium content and experiences for audiences worldwide. Driven by iconic studios, networks and streaming services, Paramount's portfolio of consumer brands includes CBS, Showtime Networks, Paramount Pictures, Nickelodeon, MTV, Comedy Central, BET, Paramount+, and Pluto TV, among others. Paramount delivers the largest share of the US television audience and boasts one of the industry's most important and extensive libraries of TV and film titles. In addition to offering innovative streaming services and digital video products, the company provides powerful capabilities in production, distribution and advertising solutions.

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

Hiring Salary Range: $56,000.00 - 80,000.00.

The hiring salary range for this position applies to New York City, California, Colorado, Washington state, and most other geographies. Starting pay for the successful applicant depends on a variety of job-related factors, including but not limited to geographic location, market demands, experience, training, and education. The benefits available for this position include medical, dental, vision, 401(k) plan, life insurance coverage, disability benefits, tuition assistance program and PTO or, if applicable, as otherwise dictated by the appropriate Collective Bargaining Agreement.

What We Offer:

• Attractive compensation and comprehensive benefits packages. Check out our full list of benefits here: https://www.paramount.com/careers/benefits

• Generous paid time off.

• An exciting and fulfilling opportunity to be part of one of Paramount's most dynamic teams.

• Opportunities for both on-site and virtual engagement events.

• Unique opportunities to make meaningful connections and build a vibrant community, both inside and outside the workplace.

• Explore life at Paramount: https://www.paramount.com/careers/life-at-paramount

Paramount is an equal opportunity employer (EOE) including disability/vet.

job_description: Are you ready to transform your passion for words into a creative career from the comfort of your own home? Whether it’s crafting snappy social media posts, refining web content, or shaping brand narratives, each task is an opportunity to flex your creative muscles. This role is more than just a job; it’s a gateway to discovering your potential as a storyteller in the digital age. Ready to put pen to paper (or fingers to keyboard) and start your journey? Your unique perspective and curiosity will fuel the creation of impactful, innovative content
Whether it’s crafting snappy social media posts, refining web content, or shaping brand narratives, each task is an opportunity to flex your creative muscles
Roles: In this role, you'll support businesses by helping them communicate effectively online through clear, consistent content
Your assignments may include writing blog entries, drafting short updates for websites or social platforms, responding to basic written messages, or organizing simple email replies
Each task is guided with step-by-step instructions, making it easy to follow—no advanced writing skills required

evaluation_reason: This entry-level 'Jr. Copywriter' role explicitly states 'no advanced writing skills required' and prefers US candidates, which fundamentally misaligns with Natalie's 13+ years of experience as a strategic conversion copywriter and launch architect, her high-quality writing aspirations, and British time zone preference.

job_description:
Have you ever stumbled upon a mobile game that swept you off your feet, leaving you eager to explore every level? As a Beginner Copywriter specializing in long-form content, your journey will begin with testing and playing these captivating apps, diving deep into their mechanics and narratives. This role invites you to analyze gameplay experiences and convey your findings through engaging copy that resonates with fellow gamers. Discover the intricacies of mobile gaming while honing your writing skills in a dynamic environment, where your insights can help broaden the horizons of another player's adventure.

Roles: In this role, you’ll explore mobile apps directly on your smartphone or tablet to understand how they function in real-world use. You’ll provide brief, honest feedback describing how intuitive the app feels, how it performs, and any areas where it could be improved.

Contract length: No fixed term — continue at your own pace and convenience.

Rate: $35 per hour.

Skills/background needed: You’ll need access to a smartphone or tablet with stable internet. Comfort with downloading apps, following simple instructions, and managing your own time is key. Expect to spend around 5 hours per week. No experience required — a great starting point for beginners in remote work.

Location: Remote work worldwide (United States preferred).

This opportunity offers a relaxed way to get involved in app evaluation. You’ll play a role in improving everyday mobile tools while enjoying the flexibility to work from anywhere, whenever it fits your schedule.

Apply today to begin turning your insights into valuable input that helps build better apps for everyone.

Apply Now
evaluation_reason: This 'Beginner Copywriter' role is a severe mismatch for your 13+ years of strategic, revenue-generating experience in funnels and launches. The content niche (mobile games) and quality standard (app reviews) are also entirely misaligned with your expertise and aspirations.

job_description:
Summary
• Position: Full-time Copywriter

• Location: Must live in the US permanently

• Salary: $120,000 - $200,000

• Equity: 0.05% - 0.25%

• Benefits: Health, Dental, Vision

• PTO: Flexible

Our Mission: Make optimal health humanity's new baseline.

This job is right for you if you:
• Live and work in the U.S. and communicate clearly in English.

• Can do this job without the support of AI.
• To be clear, we are not looking for a prompt expert, we are looking for a designer.

• Have a proven track record as a brand or content copywriter with experience crafting copy that builds culture and drives growth.

• Have a portfolio of work that you can share and describe in detail.

• Can take complex ideas and make them simple, human, and emotionally resonant.

• Know how to flex between storytelling, product copy, and social content.

• Are fluent in digital culture you know what captures attention across web, email, and social media.

• Have experience writing for wellness, performance, or lifestyle brands (bonus if you live that lifestyle).

• Move fast, give clear feedback, and can ship high-quality work without getting stuck in perfectionist wormholes.

• Are able to collaborate visually with designers and strategists, rather than simply handing off text.

• Are obsessed with clarity, persuasion, and brand voice consistency.

• Are comfortable working in a fast-paced startup where procedures are not clearly defined.

• Do the writing yourself and don’t outsource to someone on Upwork, etc.

What you'll do:
• Write clear, compelling copy for the Rythm brand across digital and physical touchpoints.

• Develop and refine Rythm’s voice, tone, and messaging architecture from web to product to campaigns.

• Create emotionally intelligent copy that inspires action without sounding clinical or corporate.

• Conceptualize and script creative campaigns, brand videos, and social storytelling moments.

• Collaborate with design and marketing teams to create cohesive content that’s visually and verbally aligned.

• Write email, ad, and landing page copy that converts and is grounded in insight and data.

• Support thought leadership, blog content, and ambassador storytelling.

• Audit existing materials and elevate language to a higher brand standard.

Hiring process:
• 30-minute intro call with Kelsie (Co-Founder & Lead Designer); chat about Rythm, the role, and your goals.

• 2-hour take-home project, i.e. write a short campaign or product copy brief.

• 30-minute call to meet Robby (Co-Founder & CEO).

• Reference checks.

Note: AI resumes will not be accepted.
evaluation_reason: Despite an excellent match in core copywriting skills, content niche, and aesthetic, this role is a critical mismatch due to the mandatory US residency and implied US time zone, directly contradicting the need for a remote, British time zone-aligned position.
